title: endiandra anthropophagorum
intro: Endiandra anthropophagorum, also known as the 'Sandpaper Tree', is a plant species native to the rainforests of New Guinea. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Endiandra anthropophagorum include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or consumed orally for its medicinal benefits.
